"cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:H/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 9.2, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:N/VI:H/VA:N/SC:N/SI:H/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-682", "name": "Incorrect Calculation"}], "description": "Zebra's block validator undercounts transparent signature operations against the 20000-sigop block limit (`MAX_BLOCK_SIGOPS`), allowing it to accept blocks that `zcashd` rejects with `bad-blk-sigops`. A miner who produces such a block can split the network: Zebra nodes follow the offending chain while `zcashd` nodes do not.

Two distinct undercounts:

#### A: Coinbase Hidden Legacy Sigops

`zcashd`'s `GetLegacySigOpCount()` includes the coinbase input's `scriptSig`. Zebra's `Sigops` impl skipped the coinbase input entirely, so up to ~98 sigops (the 100-byte coinbase script length cap, less the height prefix) could be hidden inside the coinbase `scriptSig` without being charged against the block limit.

#### B: Aggregate P2SH Sigops.

`zcashd`'s `GetP2SHSigOpCount()` parses each P2SH input's redeem script with `accurate=true` and sums those sigops into the block-wide total via `ConnectBlock`. The check is per-block, not per-transaction, and the limit applies regardless of who mines the offending block — a miner just needs to include enough P2SH-spending transactions whose redeem scripts together exceed 20000 sigops. Zebra computed P2SH sigops only on the mempool-acceptance path (used for ZIP-317 weighting) and never accumulated them during block validation. A block whose aggregate redeem-script sigops exceed 20000 (e.g. 1334 P2SH spends × 15 sigops = 20010) would be accepted by Zebra and rejected by `zcashd`.

### Patches

Fixed in this release:  https://github.com/ZcashFoundation/zebra/releases/tag/v4.4.0.

### Workarounds

None. Operators relying on Zebra for consensus should upgrade.

### Resources

- `MAX_BLOCK_SIGOPS` constant inherited from Bitcoin via the Zcash protocol spec's §7.6 catch-all "Other rules inherited from Bitcoin", tracked for explicit documentation in [zcash/zips#568](https://github.com/zcash/zips/issues/568).
- `zcashd` `GetLegacySigOpCount`: <https://github.com/zcash/zcash/blob/v6.11.0/src/main.cpp#L826-L836>
- `zcashd` `GetP2SHSigOpCount`: <https://github.com/zcash/zcash/blob/v6.11.0/src/main.cpp#L840-L852>
- `zcashd` `ConnectBlock` aggregates per-tx sigops and compares against `MAX_BLOCK_SIGOPS`.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00283, "percentile": 0.19824}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-jv4h-j224-23cc", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-07T20:54:33Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jv4h-j224-23cc", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-jv4h-j224-23cc"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-44498"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-08T15:17:01Z", "published_at": "2026-05-07T20:54:33Z", "references": ["https://github.com/ZcashFoundation/zebra/security/advisories/GHSA-jv4h-j224-23cc", "https://github.com/ZcashFoundation/zebra/releases/tag/v4.4.0", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-44498", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-jv4h-j224-23cc"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/ZcashFoundation/zebra/security-advisories/GHSA-jv4h-j224-23cc", "severity": "critical", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/ZcashFoundation/zebra", "summary": "Zebra's Block Validator Undercounts Coinbase and P2SH Sigops", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-13T13:28:48Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-jv4h-j224-23cc", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "4.4.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "rust", "name": "zebrad"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 4.4.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Zebra's Block Validator Undercounts Coinbase and P2SH Sigops
